title: tephrosia frutescens
intro: Tephrosia frutescens, also known as the fish poison tree or wild dodonaea, is a plant species native to Africa and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tephrosia frutescens include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anthelmintic to expel intestinal worms. In herbal preparations, Tephrosia frutescens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
